name: e08 - Cash Purchase and Float Reimbursement

nodes.name: Buy on cash float
nodes.documentation: Cash buyer pays the showroom from the petty-cash float and collects the goods + cash receipt/voucher. Records purchase: item, quantity, cashPrice, vendor name, receipt attachment. Permission payments.cashFloat.manage (conditional: cash buyer). No PO is created. [SCOR S2.7 | source: AB cash buy]

nodes.name: Raise cash GRN (NO PO ref)
nodes.documentation: Stores raise a cash GRN to bring the goods into inventory WITHOUT a PO reference (the distinguishing feature of the cash branch: poId = null). GRN fields: item, quantity, vendor, cashReceiptRef, actualDeliveryDate, valuation = cashPrice. grn.raise permission. QC applies if the item requires it (same hard block as credit). [SCOR S2.4 | ISO 8.6 | source: AB cash GRN no PO ref]

nodes.name: Request float reimbursement
nodes.documentation: Cash buyer raises a reimbursement request = sum of spent vouchers since last top-up (to restore the float to its allotment). Attaches all cash receipts/vouchers as evidence. permission payments.cashFloat.manage. [SCOR S2.7 | source: AB reimbursement request]

nodes.name: Finance prepares reimbursement (maker)
nodes.documentation: Finance-Maker verifies the vouchers against the cash GRNs and float ledger, prepares the reimbursement (amount = allotment - currentBalance). permission payments.cashFloat.manage (reimburse). Maker prepares but does not release (SoD). [SCOR S2.7 | source: AB + maker/checker SoD]

nodes.name: Finance approves reimbursement (checker)
nodes.documentation: Finance-Checker (Chief Accountant) reviews and approves the reimbursement before disbursement; maker != checker (financial-control SoD per SOX/COSO + ISO 37001). permission payments.cashFloat.manage (approve). Hold-with-reason if vouchers do not reconcile. [SCOR S2.7 | source: AB approval + SoD]

nodes.name: Log receipt + deduct from float balance
nodes.documentation: System logs the cash receipt against the cash GRN and deducts cashFloat.balance -= cashPrice. Writes an audit entry (category payment/cash); the cash spend is tagged so analytics can report it ALONGSIDE credit spend (unified spend view). Emits SSE. [SCOR S2.7/OE4 | source: AB cash log + deduct + unified reporting]

nodes.name: Top up float to allotment
nodes.documentation: System disburses the reimbursement and sets cashFloat.balance += reimbursementAmount (restored to allotment). Audit entry (category payment), SSE; reports update the unified cash-vs-credit spend view. [SCOR S2.7/OE4 | source: AB top-up + reporting]

nodes.name: Inquire showroom availability
nodes.documentation: Cash buyer contacts the showroom/vendor to confirm the item is in stock and the price. Captures item, quantity, expected cash price. No RFQ, no quote comparison (cash buys are small and immediate). [SCOR S1.2 | source: AB showroom inquiry]

nodes.name: Confirm stock + price
nodes.documentation: Showroom/vendor confirms availability and the cash price. If not available the buyer tries another showroom or escalates to the Head to switch to credit. [SCOR S1.2 | source: AB]

nodes.name: Head assigns cash or credit?
nodes.documentation: Expression: purchaseMode == CASH (Head set it at assignment) AND purchaseType == Local. CASH -> petty-cash float branch (this diagram), small/urgent showroom buys with no PO. CREDIT -> normal PO branch (overview + 05). Only the Head sets the mode; the cash buyer cannot self-elect cash. [SCOR S1.1 | source: AB head decides cash vs credit]

nodes.name: Cash price <= available float balance?
nodes.documentation: Expression: cashPrice <= cashFloat.balance. The cash float is a configurable petty-cash allotment per cash buyer (e.g. OMR 300; amount configurable per tenant/buyer). True -> buy on float. False -> cannot buy on float; request a reimbursement/top-up first or route the buy to credit. [SCOR S2.7 | source: AB cash float configurable amount]

nodes.name: Float low (<= ~1/3 of allotment)?
nodes.documentation: Expression: cashFloat.balance <= cashFloat.allotment * (1/3) (configurable low-water threshold, default one third remaining, e.g. <= OMR 100 of a 300 allotment). True -> request reimbursement to top the float back up. False -> done for this buy; the buyer continues with the remaining float. [SCOR S2.7 | source: AB float-low ~1/3 reimbursement trigger]

nodes.name: Approved local need to fulfil
nodes.documentation: Trigger: an approved local-purchase need is assigned for fulfilment. This branch is LOCAL ONLY (no import; imports always go credit + PO). The decision cash-vs-credit is made by the Purchase Head at assignment. [SCOR S1.1 | source: AB local purchase cash branch]

nodes.name: Goods in stock; float maintained; spend reported
nodes.documentation: Cash buy complete: goods received via cash GRN (no PO), float deducted and (if low) reimbursed and topped up, cash spend reported alongside credit spend. [SCOR S2.7 | source: AB]

nodes.name: Route to credit / PO branch (out of scope here)
nodes.documentation: Expression: purchaseMode == CREDIT. The buy follows the normal sourcing + PO branch (overview, 04, 05): RFQ/award (or framework call-off), PO issued with budget hard-commit, GRN against the PO, three-way match. Shown here only as the alternative branch of the Head's cash-vs-credit decision; detailed elsewhere. [SCOR S1.2-S2.1 | source: AB credit branch]
